mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold, restrictive monetary policy increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative pressure on equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 5.0% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls growth indicates a robust economy and strong corporate earnings potential, driving positive equity returns. This is an always-on signal reflecting underlying economic strength.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a rank information coefficient below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of slowing real retail sales (a direct measure of consumer demand) and a rising unemployment rate signals an imminent recession, causing a sharp decline in equity prices as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: When the specified trigger condition is met, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of subsequent periods over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing and business investment, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown and lower corporate profits.
failure_condition: A negative 6-month change in INDPRO is not followed by negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns over a walk-forward test covering a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation, monetary tightening regime, an upside surprise in the CPI print increases the probability of further rate hikes, negatively impacting equity valuations through a higher discount rate.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS level indicates a tightening regime
failure_condition: Positive CPI surprises (actual > consensus) are followed by positive S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time during periods where FEDFUNDS > 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from a cyclical low is a highly reliable, short-term leading indicator of a recession (the 'Sahm Rule' concept), triggering immediate risk-off sentiment in equity markets.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.3pp above its 12-month low.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 has positive returns over the subsequent 5 trading days in a majority of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downturn in housing starts, a key interest-rate sensitive and cyclical sector, precedes broader economic weakness and negatively impacts forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 6-month percentage change in HOUST fails to show a statistically significant (p < 0.10) positive rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (broad demand) decelerates concurrently with a contraction in industrial production (supply), it confirms a synchronized economic slowdown, leading to negative revisions in earnings expectations and lower stock prices.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 1.0% annualized AND INDPRO year-over-year growth < 0%
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the median 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is non-negative over a full business cycle.
